tavily.answer: As of August 7, 2026, southern Madagascar is experiencing severe drought, exacerbated by conflict and security issues. The United Nations’ food agencies have warned that acute hunger is set to worsen across 13 global hotspots, including southern Madagascar, due to conflict, funding shortages, and climate shocks. The country is under military rule since October 2025 and has intensified its military cooperation with Russia, receiving a second shipment of military hardware to upgrade its engineering corps. The humanitarian situation is dire, with millions facing high levels of acute food insecurity and famine risks rising.

items.title: Ramaphosa vows crackdown on xenophobic violence - Reuters
